title: Revise UX for Metrics Analyst Dashboard Selection and Results
story: As a DataTools Pro user running the Metrics Analyst, I want a clear, guided dashboard selection and results review flow, so that I always know what step I am on and can efficiently review recommendations.
description:
A comprehensive UX revision of the Metrics Analyst dashboard selection and results flow, addressing clarity, navigation, and visual consistency.

### Dashboard Selection

- Default view updated to a cleaner layout
- Selected dashboard slides down to reveal a summary with a "Step 1:..." button label guiding the user to the next action
- Report links styled in Salesforce blue with the cloud icon to indicate they open in Salesforce

### Processing Experience

- Single consolidated processing message: "This batch process takes about 1 minute per dashboard"
- Subsequent rotating messages appended after a delay (processing meta-data, generating definitions, etc.)

### Results Display

- Default view after processing shows the Suggested Metrics tab
- Added bottom margin and scrollbar to the widget popup and results tables so all items are accessible
- Selected summary box gets a visual outline connecting it to the detail table below
- Added section titles to clarify the action context for each result group
- Add and Merge buttons moved to the right side with gray styling instead of light green
- Added a Definition column to the results table
- Removed the Classification column (deprecated)

### Salesforce Link Styling

- All links that open into Salesforce use the Salesforce blue color with the cloud icon, applied consistently across the app.
acceptance_criteria: Default Metrics Analyst view shows the updated clean layout
Selecting a dashboard slides down to show the summary with a 'Step 1' button
Report links display in Salesforce blue with the cloud icon
Processing shows a single consolidated message initially
Rotating detail messages append after a delay during processing
Results default to the Suggested Metrics tab after processing completes
Widget popup and results tables have a scrollbar with max height
Selected summary box has a visual outline connecting to the detail table
Section titles are visible for each result group
Add and Merge buttons are on the right side with gray styling
Definition column is present in the results table
Classification column is removed from the results table
Salesforce link styling is applied consistently across the app
ux_changes: Comprehensive UX revision: updated default layout, added slide-down dashboard summary with step labels, Salesforce blue link styling with cloud icons, consolidated processing message with delayed rotating detail messages, default to Suggested Metrics after processing, scrollbars on widget/results tables, visual outline on selected summary box, section titles, repositioned Add/Merge buttons with gray styling, added Definition column, removed deprecated Classification column.
implementation_notes: The Definition column could not be populated immediately because Service 2 did not return a definition field at the time. A follow-up ticket was created to enhance Service 2 to include the definition. The 'Metric Description (LLM)' field from the API response was identified as the closest candidate. Salesforce blue link styling was applied globally across the app, not just in the Analyst.
comment_summary: Developer implemented all UX changes and deployed to development. Flagged that the Definition column lacked a data source since Service 2 does not return a 'definition' field — only 'Metric Description (LLM)'. Ryan acknowledged and created a follow-up ticket to enhance Service 2. Ticket was closed with the Definition column enhancement deferred.
